title: eryngium chamissonis
intro: Eryngium chamissonis, also known as sea eryngo or beach eryngo, is a coastal plant species in the genus Eryngium, characterized by its blue or purple flowers and spiny leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Eryngium chamissonis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and wounds.
